reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement. The IVP hypothesis `hf` is equivalent to the informal IVP condition: the Lean version requires `a < b` and uses a disjunction `(f a < c ∧ c < f b) ∨ (f b < c ∧ c < f a)`, which together cover exactly the same cases as the symmetric informal condition "if f(a) < c < f(b), then ∃x between a and b with f(x)=c" (the case a=b makes the informal hypothesis contradictory, and a>b is covered by swapping to the second disjunct). The closed-set hypothesis `hg : ∀ r : ℚ, IsClosed {x | f x = r}` matches "for every rational r, the set of all x with f(x)=r is closed" via the standard ℚ→ℝ coercion. The conclusion `Continuous f` is the standard Mathlib notion of continuity on ℝ. No extra hypotheses are added and none are dropped.
